title: One Dead and Several Injured in Tent Collapse Outside Virginia Church
description: Severe weather conditions caused the collapse during an outdoor service and concert Friday night.
author: Madaleine Rubin

title: FISA, a Key U.S. Spying Power, Is Expiring. What Does That Mean for Foreign Surveillance?
description: Top officials and some lawmakers say letting a powerful spying authority expire on Saturday will leave the United States dangerously blind. But surveillance can still continue.
author: Dustin Volz

title: 26-Year Mystery of a Skeleton in a Tent Ends With DNA Identification
description: Remains of the man, Joseph Louis Serrao Jr., discovered in a remote area in Olympic National Park in 2000, were identified using forensic genealogy.
author: Christine Hauser

title: Judge Dismisses Republican Group’s Case Against the University of Florida
description: A campus group the university banned after accusations of antisemitic behavior failed to prove it had its First Amendment rights violated, the court ruled.
author: Stephanie Saul

title: Two Dan Sullivans? Alaska Republicans Suggest Kicking One Off the Ballot in Senate Race
description: The lieutenant governor and the top elections official, both Republicans, are investigating whether the challenger coordinated with a Democrat to confuse voters.
author: Tim Balk and Kellen Browning

title: Musk Is the World’s First Trillionaire. Who Was the First Billionaire?
description: John D. Rockefeller, the Gilded Age oil baron, became America’s first billionaire in 1916. Like Elon Musk’s ascension, the milestone made headlines.
author: John S.W. MacDonald

title: Judge Blocks National Parks From Removing ‘Negative’ Signs and Depictions of Slavery
description: The ruling accused the Trump administration of engaging in censorship by taking down materials at parks across the country.
author: Maxine Joselow

title: World Cup Ticket Prices in Mexico City Too Expensive for Most Fans
description: The opening game in Mexico City took place amid weeks of protests from groups including teachers’ unions complaining about pay and pensions.
author: Tariq Panja

title: Trump Administration Says It Will Restart Asylum and Immigration Processing
description: The response came after a federal judge rebuked officials for failing to immediately comply with the order he issued last week.
author: Madeleine Ngo and Zach Montague

title: Suspect in Fatal Shooting Is Dead After a Standoff in Midland, Texas, Officials Say
description: The man was confirmed to be dead after he had barricaded himself in a building in Midland, following an earlier shooting on Friday that killed one person and injured 10 others.
author: Mack Liederman

title: Despite Trump Claims, Spencer Pratt Seems to Accept L.A. Mayor Results
description: President Trump said the loss was an example of election fraud, but Mr. Pratt did not entertain that idea. Instead, he plans to wind down his campaign but keep attacking the two Democrats who advanced.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: Ohio City Worker Union Complains That Goats Are Eating Its Lunch
description: A union in Columbus made a formal complaint after the city farmed out brush-clearing duties at a wastewater plant. The competitors? Goats.
author: Sejal Govindarao

title: Gavin Newsom and Hunter Biden Would Like Your Attention, Please
description: The two men chatted on Mr. Newsom’s podcast about Democrats, Joe Biden, Graham Platner, phone addiction and other topics.
author: Reid J. Epstein

title: Trump’s Haphazard Midterm Leadership Puts Republicans in a Bind
description: The president and his party have had a rough start to June, after a spring full of MAGA primary victories and redistricting gains.
author: Katie Glueck

title: To Defeat Democrats, Texas Governor Embraces the Hard Right
description: After years of keeping his distance, Gov. Greg Abbott is set to address Texas Republicans’ activist-led convention as hard-line conservatives gain influence within the party.
author: Lauren McGaughy

title: Federal Agents Search Voting Rights Group in Ohio
description: It was not immediately clear what the investigators were looking for, but a board member with a progressive group said members had been served with search warrants.
author: Devlin Barrett and Glenn Thrush

title: Rick Jackson, Georgia Governor Candidate, Is Also a Film Producer Battling the IRS
description: Rick Jackson, a billionaire, wants to be Georgia’s next governor. But the I.R.S. has questioned how his low-budget movies turned into tax windfalls.
author: Danny Hakim

title: Judge Declines to Halt UFC Fight at the White House on Trump’s Birthday
description: In a ruling on Friday, Judge Amit P. Mehta wrote that the lawsuit arrived last minute and failed to show how the event irreversibly harmed the individuals who sued.
author: Zach Montague

title: Atlanta Judge Eleanor Ross Says There Is ‘No Excuse’ For Her Misconduct
description: In a second apology letter, Judge Eleanor Ross said her actions were “patently wrong.” Some House Republicans want her impeached.
author: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs and Mattathias Schwartz

title: Blocking Trump’s Fund, Judge Presses for Guarantee It Won’t Move Forward
description: The ruling was the strongest effort to date to hold the administration to its word that its plans to create the fund have actually been set aside.
author: Alan Feuer
